JOURNAL (current thesis state):
# Journal — Investing Agent

## 1. Market view

Universe broadly weak heading into the July earnings cluster; cash at 66.4% reflects patience rather than conviction. Seven positions across five sectors, all starter-to-moderate size. The next 40 days are the critical adjudication window: ATRLJ ~Jul 10, CORE-B ~Jul 22, VOLV/ESSITY ~Jul 24, RUSTA Q1 TBD, ASSA/THULE ~Jul 28–29. No urgency to add before those prints resolve. Rusta initiated this week as the cleanest new signal; ASSA-B deferred for a better entry post-Q2.

## 2. Holdings

**HM-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.9%
- **Thesis:** Karl-Johan Persson bought ~1.2 BSEK across six transactions in eight days into an 11% drawdown — one of the strongest insider signals possible on Swedish Large Cap. Starter position pending fundamental confirmation.
- **Sell if:** Profit warning, gross margin collapse, or confirmed structural market-share loss to Shein/Temu. Not on price weakness alone.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ESSITY-B.ST** — Core | Consumer Staples | 8.0%
- **Thesis:** Exceptional 90-day insider cluster — 512 MSEK, 7 buyers, 0 sellers — led by Industrivärden. Defensive staples compounder at ~14x P/E with 3.4% yield. Q2 ~Jul 24 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Sustained margin deterioration where input costs structurally exceed pricing power, or Q2 shows revenue decline accelerating with margin compression.
- **Status:** `intact`

**THULE.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 3.5%
- **Thesis:** Ten insiders including chair, vice chair, CEO, and CFO bought ~14–25 MSEK across two waves. Quality mid-cap franchise 28% off highs; weakness is cyclical not structural. Q2 ~Jul 29 is the next adjudicator.
- **Sell if:** Insider turns net seller, operating margin compresses below ~13%, or Q2 reveals genuine earnings deterioration rather than cyclical softness.
- **Status:** `intact`

**VOLV-B.ST** — Core | Industrials | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Industrivärden deployed ~290 MSEK in a single day near 52-week highs. Net cash ~58 BSEK, ~4% yield, 9.8% operating margin. Controlling owner buying near highs endorses forward earnings. Q2 ~Jul 24.
- **Sell if:** Industrivärden becomes net seller, profit warning, or Q2 reveals order intake collapse rather than cyclical normalisation.
- **Status:** `intact`

**ATRLJ-B.ST** — Core | Real Estate | 4.8%
- **Thesis:** Tagehus (controlling family) bought ~30 MSEK into Varma's forced 7%-discount block exit. Ericsson 58k sqm Hagastaden anchor lease provides fundamental support. Starter size given net debt/equity 101%. Q2 ~Jul 10.
- **Sell if:** Further forced-seller overhang without insider response; Q2 reveals deteriorating occupancy/lease renewals; or Ericsson lease proves less economically material than signalled.
- **Status:** `intact`

**CORE-B.ST** — Aggressive | Real Estate | 2.9%
- **Thesis:** Rutger Arnhult bought ~110 MSEK personally across two waves into a 50%-off, capitulating leveraged commercial real estate name. FCF positive (678 MSEK R12); this is a leverage/rate story, not a going-concern story. Q2 ~Jul 22.
- **Sell if:** Dilutive equity raise, covenant breach, LTV deterioration in Q2, or Arnhult becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

**RUSTA.ST** — Core | Consumer Discretionary | 4.5%
- **Thesis:** Controlling owner Aforber Invest bought ~52 MSEK across 13 transactions (including a single 30.6 MSEK ticket) days after a positive Q4 print and dividend raise, into a -23% drawdown. Discount retail has structural counter-cyclical tailwind. Starter size pending verification of the D/E 243% leverage (likely IFRS-16-driven but unconfirmed).
- **Sell if:** Leverage confirmed as genuine financial debt rather than operating leases and the deleveraging path is unclear; same-store sales deteriorate; or Aforber Invest becomes a net seller.
- **Status:** `intact`

## 3. Watchlist

**ASSA-B.ST** — Broad insider cluster (CEO ~35 MSEK + 4 operating managers) across April–June. Q2 Jul 28 is the gate. Target entry ≤340 SEK; Latour block-sale overhang may deliver it. Revisit post-print or on pullback below 335.

**VIMIAN.ST** — Fidelio Vet + CEO bought ~128 MSEK over two weeks; signal is real but anchor-holder concentrated and FCF is -243 MEUR. Q2 ~Jul 29. Analyst would-buy-at 27 SEK; watch into the print, reassess fundamentals then.

**NOLA-B.ST** — Principal owner Herenco bought ~25 MSEK in three clustered June purchases; clean signal endorsing the CEO transition. Revenue declining multi-year (-3.2% YoY). Entry target ~47 SEK; prefer to wait for Q2 (Aug 5) print under new CEO before sizing.

**LIFCO-B.ST** — Strong insider cluster (3 distinct buyers, board member 2x, ~10 MSEK around Q1 print) on a quality compounder. Price at 307 is above the 275–285 entry zone. Wait for Q2 (~Jul 24) pullback or post-print confirmation; re-screen week of Jul 21.

**HEXA-B.ST** — 4 management buyers including CFO bought ~12.4 MSEK into a -20% drawdown; no sellers. Data quality flag: fundamentals block contains implausible values (P/E 0.9, FCF yield 154%) — cannot validate valuation. Q2 ~Jul 25. Starter defensible on insider signal alone if data is cleaned up; otherwise wait for print.

## 4. Lessons learned / open questions

- **Rusta fill vs limit:** PM set limit at 86 SEK, execution filled at 75.50 — meaningfully better entry. No lesson to change, but note that the market offered a 12% better price than the limit; the conservative limit was correct given elevated vol (48.6%).
- **Consumer Discretionary concentration:** HM + Thule + Rusta = ~13% of portfolio. No further Consumer Discretionary adds without rebalancing one of these, even if the insider signal is strong. The sector cap (25%) is not close, but three correlated names is enough.
- **Correlated real estate:** ATRLJ + CORE-B = ~7.7%, both leveraged Swedish commercial real estate. No further real estate adds without rebalancing one first.
- **Open question — earnings cluster:** ATRLJ (Jul 10), CORE-B (Jul 22), VOLV/ESSITY (Jul 24), ASSA/THULE (Jul 28–29) all land in a 19-day window. Consider whether any pre-print trimming is warranted, or whether current starter sizing provides sufficient cushion. Reassess at next weekly cycle once ATRLJ reports first.

ANALYST NOTES on new (not-yet-held) names surfaced today:
# Securitas (SECU-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Securitas is a global security services leader trading at ~16x P/E with a 9.4% FCF yield and 3.4% dividend — a defensive, cash-generative compounder. The current setup is muddied: a large block sale from anchor owner Latour (~5 BSEK in late May) likely caps near-term upside, while insider activity inside the company is mixed but not bearish on net. Interesting as a watch, not a clear buy today.

**Positives**
- Strong FCF generation: 8.4 BSEK FCF, 9.4% FCF yield — high-quality earnings backing the 3.4% dividend.
- CFO and several "other management" insiders bought in early June at prices similar to today — a quiet vote of confidence at this level.
- CEO Ahlqvist's late-June activity, while net-flat in SEK terms, shows he was a buyer first (Jun 18) before selling (Jun 22) — pattern consistent with option/program mechanics rather than directional bearishness, though I can't verify that.
- Defensive sector (security services), low-volatility name (30d vol 23.6%), reasonable valuation, near 200MA — not stretched.

**Concerns**
- Revenue -6.5% YoY on R12 — top line is going the wrong way; I cannot tell from this data whether it's FX, divestments, or organic weakness. This matters and I'd want to read the Q1 report before sizing a position.
- Net debt 32 BSEK, net debt/equity 77.5% — manageable given FCF but not a fortress balance sheet.
- Operating margin 6.1% and net 3.6% are thin — typical for the industry but leaves little buffer if revenue keeps sliding.
- **Latour sold 5 BSEK on May 26** — that's a major long-term holder reducing. Overhang risk: if they sell more, the stock has a ceiling. This is the dominant signal in the tape right now.

**Insider read.** Mixed but tilted constructive on the company-internal side: CFO + several managers bought in early June, CEO net roughly flat. **However**, the Latour block sale (external 5 BSEK reduction) outweighs the internal buying in market-impact terms. Net: neutral-to-slightly-cautious.

**Price read.** Up 12.7% over 12m, +3.7% over 3m, flat over 1m, sitting 3.3% above the 200MA and 9.8% below the 52w high. Consolidating after a run — no urgency to chase, no break to fade.

**Verdict.** WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist.

The fundamentals are defensible and the insider tape is fine, but the Latour overhang plus the unexplained revenue decline argue for waiting. The Aug 4 earnings report (~41 days) will clarify the revenue trend and likely flush remaining Latour selling. Re-evaluate then, or earlier if the stock retests the 200MA (~150 SEK) on volume.

If we want exposure before the print, wait for a retest of ~150 (200MA) on weakness. CEO's Jun 18 buy / Jun 22 sell pattern looks program-driven; I'd not over-read it either way."
```json
}
```

# Viking Supply (VSSAB-B.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** Viking Supply shows strong revenue growth (+40% YoY) and unusually high gross margins (88%) for an Industrials/First North name, hinting this may be a capital-light or service/IP-driven business rather than a traditional shipping operator. However, deeply negative FCF (–947 MSEK, –51% yield) and rising net debt (73% of equity) suggest the growth is being bought, not earned. Without more business context, I cannot tell whether this is healthy expansion investment or cash-burn dressed up as growth.

**Positives**
- Revenue growth is genuinely strong: +40% YoY on a 17% 3-year CAGR — accelerating, not decelerating.
- Gross margin of 88% is striking for an Industrials classification and suggests pricing power or a misclassified business model worth understanding.
- Price action is constructive: +20% over 12m, holding near 52w high (–3.8%), trading 13% above 200-day MA — market is not signalling distress.
- Operating margin of 19.8% indicates the business *can* convert revenue to profit at the operating line.

**Concerns**
- FCF of –947 MSEK against revenue of 1,028 MSEK is alarming — the business burned nearly a year of revenue in free cash flow. I cannot determine from the data whether this reflects heavy capex for growth or working-capital deterioration.
- Net debt at 1,455 MSEK and 73% of equity is high, and with negative FCF the trajectory is the wrong way. Refinancing risk is a real consideration I cannot size.
- ROE of just 5.4% despite a 19.8% operating margin implies either heavy share count, large equity base, or weak below-the-line economics — something doesn't reconcile cleanly.
- P/E of 17 on a First North name with negative FCF and rising leverage is not a margin-of-safety valuation.
- 30-day volatility of 45% confirms First North risk profile — position sizing must respect this.
- No insider activity and no news in 30d at materiality 3+, so I have nothing to triangulate the "daily trigger" angle the Screener mentioned — I cannot see what surfaced this name today.

**Insider read.** No conviction-grade insider transactions in 90 days. Neutral — no support, no warning.

**Price read.** Strong but extended: near 52w high, well above 200-day MA, with significant 12m gains already realised. Entering here is buying into strength, not a setup.

**Verdict.** The growth and margin profile are interesting enough to track, but the FCF/leverage picture is a serious red flag I cannot resolve without seeing the cash-flow statement detail and understanding what this business actually does. This is a "I need to know more before I'd buy" situation, not an actionable idea today. `WATCH — not actionable now, keep on watchlist`

```json
  "would_sell_at": null,

Before any buy I'd want to understand the business (gross margin and Industrials classification don't fit) and see whether negative FCF is growth capex or working-capital deterioration. First North + 45% vol + leverage means strict aggressive-sleeve sizing if ever taken."
}
```

# Scandi Standard (SCST.ST) — Analyst Note

**Thesis.** A persistent, sizeable insider cluster — led by Portuguese poultry group Grupo Lusiaves (vice chairman seat) plus the CEO and several managers — has been buying SCST in the open market over the last 6+ weeks, totaling ~19.5 MSEK across 9 distinct buyers with zero sellers. On Swedish Mid Cap, a buy cluster of this breadth (strategic owner + CEO + management) is one of the strongest single signals we track, and it's landing into Q2 earnings (~5 weeks out).

**Positives**
- Insider signal is unusually strong: 9 buyers, 0 sellers, ~19.5 MSEK net, sustained over weeks rather than a single print — and crucially includes the strategic industrial owner *and* the CEO (Tunestål, 4.7 MSEK personally).
- Lusiaves is a poultry industrial, not a financial holder — accumulation here implies a strategic read on the business, and historically such stake-building can lead to bid speculation.
- Fundamentals are respectable for a staples mid-cap: revenue +8.7% YoY, ROE 14%, dividend yield 2.3%, and 12m price action (+47.5%) shows the market has been re-rating the name.
- Earnings catalyst ~34 days out — insiders are buying with the print in clear sight, which is itself information.

**Concerns**
- Valuation is no longer cheap: P/E 23.6 on a 4.5% operating margin / 2.8% net margin business is a full multiple for a low-margin protein processor; FCF yield is only 1.5%.
- Balance sheet carries meaningful leverage (net debt/equity 71.5%), which limits resilience if input costs (feed, energy) turn.
- Price is +22% above the 200-day MA and has run +47% in 12m — entry is into strength, not weakness, so risk/reward on a pullback is asymmetric the wrong way short-term.
- I cannot evaluate the latest quarterly margin trajectory in detail from the data I have, nor whether the Lusiaves accumulation is heading toward a formal bid threshold — both would change the verdict.

**Insider read.** Exceptionally strong. Breadth (9 buyers including strategic owner + CEO + multiple managers), size (~19.5 MSEK), persistence (6+ weeks), and zero offsetting selling — this is close to a textbook cluster buy on Swedish Mid Cap. The Lusiaves angle adds optionality (strategic intent, possible bid) on top of the conviction signal.

**Price read.** Extended: +22% above 200-day MA, 12m +47.5%, but consolidating last 1–3 months (essentially flat) — the stock has digested the run rather than blown off, which is constructive.

**Verdict.** `INTERESTING — fits Core sleeve`

Worth a Core starter position even at full valuation; the Lusiaves accumulation also carries optionality on a strategic move. Recommend sizing modestly given valuation and leverage, and revisiting after Q2 print (~2026-07-28). If Lusiaves crosses a disclosure threshold (10%/20%/30%) before then, that is a fresh trigger for the Daily PM."
```json
}
```

RISK CAPS (mirrors constitution §4 — enforced in code):
  - Max 15% single holding (of total portfolio)
  - Max 25% any single sector (of total)
  - Max 10% single Aggressive position
  - Max 20% Aggressive sleeve total
  - Min 30% cash buffer of the Core sleeve
  - Max 90% total equity exposure
  - Max ~10 holdings
  - Min 4-week holding period — early full exit requires thesis_break=True + rationale
response_text:
## Trigger read

Three new-name flags, one watchlist mover, one preview-only news item on HM-B. SECU-B and VSSAB both came back from the Analyst as WATCH — not actionable. LIFCO up 5.4% is price, not news — already noted for Sunday. HM-B "quarterly preview" is calendar noise, not the print itself. The only item with real signal density is **SCST**: a 9-buyer, 0-seller, 6-week cluster led by the strategic industrial owner *and* the CEO, into Q2 earnings ~5 weeks out. Conviction 4 from the Analyst.

## Decision

**Buy SCST.ST — Core starter, ~5%.** This is the kind of insider tape the agent exists to act on: breadth (9 distinct buyers, zero sellers), composition (strategic owner Lusiaves + CEO Tunestål personally + multiple managers), persistence (6+ weeks), and a near-term earnings adjudicator. The Lusiaves angle adds strategic optionality on top of the conviction read. Valuation is full and the chart is extended — both real concerns — which is why this is a starter (~5%), not a full position. Analyst's would-buy is 140; today's tape will set the actual fill via paper execution.

Why act today rather than wait for Sunday: the cluster has been building for weeks and is landing into a print ~34 days out. Each weekday is meaningful when an industrial owner is accumulating; if Lusiaves crosses a disclosure threshold before Sunday the entry calculus changes against us. This clears the "would Sunday be too late" bar.

**Sizing math:** 5% of 100,077 ≈ 5,000 SEK. At Analyst's reference of ~140 SEK that's ~36 shares.

**No action on the others.** SECU-B: Latour overhang + unexplained revenue decline → wait for Aug 4. VSSAB: cannot reconcile FCF with headline margins → understand the business first. LIFCO: price-only move, no event. HM-B: preview, not print.

## Risk awareness

- Adds a 6th sector exposure (Consumer Staples already has ESSITY at 8.3% → SCST adds ~5% → sector ~13%, well under 25%).
- Core sleeve cash buffer: post-trade equity ~38%, cash ~62% — well above the 30% Core-cash-buffer floor.
- Total holdings → 8, under the ~10 cap.
- No correlation issue (poultry processor, distinct from existing names).

```json
{
  "summary": "Open a 5% Core starter in SCST on a rare 9-buyer / 0-seller cluster led by strategic owner Lusiaves and the CEO into a ~34-day earnings catalyst.",
  "trades": [
    {
      "action": "buy",
      "ticker": "SCST.ST",
      "shares": 36,
      "limit_price_sek": 142.0,

      "sector": "Consumer Staples",
      "rationale": "Sustained 6-week insider cluster (9 buyers, 0 sellers, ~19.5 MSEK) including strategic industrial owner Lusiaves and CEO Tunestål personally, landing into Q2 print ~Jul 28 — signal too time-sensitive to defer to Sunday.",
      "thesis_break": false
    }
  ],
  "no_action_note": null
}
```
